reviews.text: Nathaniel Kaleefy and his team are true advocates! Plus great work product, strong presence in court, thorough communication.

I want to commend Nathaniel J. Kaleefey, Attorney as legal counselor in family law.  When my wife of 24 years wanted a separation, the last thing that I wanted to do was get advice from an attorney, yet I am so thankful that I did because from that very first consultation, as difficult as it was to hear, Nathaniel made me realize that I was allowing our children and myself to become unnecessarily hurt by the painful process of what would ultimately become a divorce.  Nathaniel did not spare my feelings and he didn’t waste our time with sentimentality, rather he spoke clearly and strongly that, as difficult as it might have been for me, I needed to think about the children. What he said about the children had benefits to me as well, but what stands out the most in that first consultation (which will be forever seared in my brain) is how he spoke about making “first things first”. About a year after that consultation, (having taken the advice he gave me) I was in a good position to endure what unfortunately turned into a scorched earth campaign against me.
In developing what would become the judgement of divorce (a legal decree that the judge signs ending the marriage), Nathaniel protected me not only from severe treatment (helping me avoid unnecessary exposure), but also, I came to find out, from significant harm in post-judgement proceedings, which, unhappily, became necessary.
Unexpectedly it was necessary to be part of a post-judgement trial and Nathaniel spoke so expertly and so strongly as my advocate that I was actually a little afraid of him as he so persuasively contended with our legal opponents.  As he advocated for me in the face of nasty and personal attacks against me, I genuinely felt like I was safe inside a fortified redoubt while my “attackers” withered under his knowledge of the law and his confident verbal offensive.  I actually saw opposing counsel stutter to find her bearing from the force of his argument while the judge’s intense scrutiny did nothing to ease her bewilderment.  Although Nathaniel had prepared me for the worst and we had the “receipts” of our reasonableness and good-faith, anyone witnessing that trial could not have come away with any other verdict than what the judge rendered.  There was simply no answer to it.  I am more convinced now than ever that the reason that he was so successful was that Nathaniel had his eyes on the ball, and from our first consultation to the post-judgement trial, literally years later, he never took his eyes off of that ball.
One of the things I have so deeply learned from this experience was the value of a solid law firm in general, and a capable legal advocate in particular.  Verspoor Waalkes PC had been recommended to me from good friends when it came time to plan the estate of my parents.  Their efficacy and kindness in the process confirmed that we had been steered in the right direction.  So when it came time to seek legal advice for myself personally, naturally they were the firm that I called.
The reason that I want to recommend Nathaniel J. Kaleefey is not so much for his sake but for the sake of whoever is reading this.  Nathaniel doesn’t need my recommendation.  He will have plenty of work because there is a severe scarcity of ethical representation in family law.  I am writing this for your sake.  Don’t get an attorney that tells you everything you want to hear.  Get one that tells you what you need to hear.  Family law is filled with attorneys that see their clients chiefly as a source of revenue rather than as people who need their expertise and advocacy.  Attorneys who have ultimately different goals than their clients cannot hope to represent those clients well, and, as was revealed by the attorney opposing us, may end up costing their clients many thousands of dollars to chase frivolous pursuits.
reviews.ownerResponse: Joe, I respect you, so this kind review means a lot to me.   It was my pleasure to get to know you and to serve you.  Thank you for trusting me with your case.  I am very happy with the process we followed and the results.  Nathaniel

reviews.text:
Navigating a divorce after 25 years of marriage was one of the most difficult experiences of my life and I am beyond grateful that I had Nathaniel Kaleefey and his remarkable team by my side. As someone who had never been through the divorce process before, I was overwhelmed, scared and uncertain about what to expect. From our very first meeting, Nathaniel immediately put me to ease. His genuine concern for my feelings, well-being and safety stood out and I could tell he sincerely cared about my case.

Nathaniel is an exceptional listener and strategist. He helped me to focus on what mattered most and his experience and tenacity ensured a favorable outcome.
Throughout the six-month process, Nathaniel's guidance was a steadying force. He approached my concerns with logic, compassion and even humor, which helped lighten the emotional burden. Recognizing my concerns about costs, he offered practical ways to reduce expenses by having me handle certain tasks. He was also incredibly respectful of my work schedule, making communication seamless and minimizing disruptions.

After the case concluded, Nathaniel ensured all documents were filed promptly and even followed up to offer further assistance. His final words of gratitude and kindness were the perfect reflection of his character and professionalism. His team, including Umberto and Sean, were also exceptional and made me feel supported every step of the way.

If you're facing the daunting process of divorce, I cannot recommend Nathaniel Kaleefey highly enough. He is not only a lawyer - he's a compassionate ally on one of life's hardest journeys.

reviews.text:
Expert, personable team with great integrity!

We never thought we would be in the position to sue someone but here we are and have learned a lot thanks to this team. After buying our first home, we discovered the previous owners covered up serious foundation issues but neglected to disclose anything on the Seller's Disclosure, therefore committing fraud. We met with multiple lawyers to hear our case, including a a consultation with Matthew Stout and he clearly stood out among the rest. He understood our situation, explained the process & several options we could take, was very informative, answered a ton of our questions, and wanted to reach a conclusion tailored to our concerns without compromising integrity. He did not pressure us into any decisions in the entire process. He gave us information-, explaining pros, cons, and potential outcomes- so we could make an informed decision on our own.

The team at Stout law took the time to understand us and our situation and truly stood for us. They represented our values and perspectives throughout the entire process when deliberating with the opposing party. The team was thorough and firm in their stance and demands to the opposing party. They are extremely knowledgeable of real estate law, including relevant case studies, which was evident all along the way. When we confronted the opposing party, the team at Stout Law expertly captured our concerns and communicated them forthright. They also were forthright with demands to reach a legal conclusion.

Matthew always gave us an opportunity to review communications before they went out to ensure we were on the same page. We had several back-and-forth communications with the opposing party and Matthew kept us involved every step of the way- not just involved/present, but participants. We feel like they truly represented us throughout the entire situation.

reviews.text:
Honestly so displeased with this experience and don't recommend this office at all.

At first everything was great and Mr. Stout was going to be such a life saver. My grandma was told she needed to have her affairs in order for her emergent heart surgery that was scheduled that day for about two weeks later. Mr. Stout heard my voicemail after 5:30 on a Friday night, and still texted me back for an appointment, which we booked for the following Thursday, which was exactly a week from her surgery. After the weekend, multiple other law offices I had called, called back telling me they could squeeze us in and have everything fit in that timetable, however I was led to believe we had everything squared away with our appointment here.

This is where my experience falls apart and becomes absolutely abysmal. Mr. Stout had the entire following week to call us and let us know his availability had changed. But rather than doing that, he called and left a voicemail at 9:30am the day of our appointment, telling us he had realized he couldn't fit us into his schedule and he was booked solid for 3 weeks. Then he recommended his friend who when we called him, well, needless to say he wasn't very kind about our situation. So now we were left scrambling to find someone, because our appointment was cancelled less than 6 HOURS before our scheduled appointment.

And that's where my issue falls. If I cancel an appointment less than 24 hours at ANY establishment, I'm typically paying for the appointment anyway. So to leave us such little notice, knowing how time sensitive our issue was? Just absolutely unprofessional in every page of the book. I don't recommend coming to this office. It would have been a different story had he given us at least a 24 hour hour notice. But again, I have absolutely never heard of such little warning, as someone who works with clients as well, you need to have a better understanding of your appointment schedule. Professionalism at it's worst here. Thankfully we were able to find assistance with one of the multiple other lawyers that I had spoken too, but just, so dumbfounded by this experience and just left with the worst taste in my mouth.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am very sorry for the short notice of our cancellation, Matthew. You are absolutely correct that you should have been given more notice. It was my sincere desire to help even though I knew that my schedule was not favorable, but I let my optimism that I could move other commitments around allow me to say yes when I should have said otherwise. My attempts to fit this work in did not come to fruition, and I felt that referring you to another firm was in your best interest. However, you should have been given more notice, and I will certainly take this constructive feedback to heart. Please accept my apology.

Randy is a fantastic lawyer and experienced trial attorney. I’ve known him for many years and highly recommend him and his team for their knowledge, dedication and care.
Knowledgeable, honest

Randy and his firm are class act. They treat you with respect and ensure you are involved in all aspects of your case. Randy is meticulous and works extremely hard to get a favorable result. I wouldn’t hesitate to use him again.

Randy was a very understanding, compassionate and hard working attorney in both of my accident claims. (I was hit as a pedestrian with a truck- and 18 months later rear ended). Randy got me the best settlements possible in both cases and I am extremely grateful for his determination to fight for more compensation in both cases. I recommend him to anyone searching for great legal representation. God forbid I need him again- but if I ever was in need- I have him on speed dial and recommend his to any and everyone who has been injured etc in an accident.

Randy and his team took a challenging personal injury case I was involved in and did an amazing job despite facing some obstacles that might have had another attorney tell me to just drop the case.  I placed my faith in him and his team the entire time during this long process and was awarded a settlement I never thought I would ever receive.  I would recommend this firm to anyone involved in a personal injury situation without any hesitation whatsoever.
Super helpful.  Always answered my questions.  Always kept me up to date on key information.  Very reachable by phone and email.  Very happy with services provided.

After leaving the medical center for treatment of my back injury, I saw a red flag immediately! The doctor mentioned my aging as a factor for the injury. I knew I needed an attorney . . but who? And where do I begin looking? I randomly made a call to an attorney who was good enough to say he couldn’t help me, but he knew another attorney who could -  Bob Bandeen. Bob is an attorney who works hard for the average employee. Workers Comp is your friend for 3 months, and then they throw you under the bus. Bob was always prompt at returning calls and emails. He’s very thorough in his research, and will listen to your thoughts and questions without making you feel dumb (if that’s the case, sometimes I had a good thought to add in the mix and Bob listened). In the end, I can highly recommend Bob Bandeen as an attorney who will fight hard for you. The office staff are all friendly and treat you with much respect. It took almost 4 years to get my settlement, but Bob was there every step of the way.

Very professional and responsive.  I had a different lawyer in beginning and wish I had bob thru the entire process.  But when bob took over he got me more than I expected and rather quick. Well communicated always giving updates on my case without me bugging him. Overall expectations met and above.
Our experience was great, every step was explained and they answered all our questions and responded quickly to all our emails and voicemail. Definitely recommend this practice.
Randy was great to work with. He always responded quickly to our questions and was able to get us a fair settlement. I would highly recommend working with him.
You never even bothered to return any of my calls so how would you even know if you could or couldn't assist me. Lol
reviews.ownerResponse: Jim, we are very sorry we did not connect. Please call us right away and we would be happy to speak with you and see if we can assist you. Thank you.

reviews.text: My wife and I hired Bob Bandeen when she was a victim of a dog attack. The owners had 3 pit bull mix large dogs on leases. One of the dogs lunged and bit her several times. Bob Bandeen was very sympathetic and a great advisor through the whole process concerning her case. He was professional, thorough and she was able to settle her case out of court in an amount that was very fair. With Bob's help and expertise, she was able to settle in an amount that was more than many people who had suffered such a trauma. It was the partnership that my wife was able to form with Bob that made all the difference. They made a great team together to win her case which she so much deserved after all her suffering. High praise for Bob Bandeen!

I contacted Mr. Allaben, who drove out to meet with my elderly parents. He spent time explaining the legal options and next steps, putting them at ease. Mr. Allaben kept us informed of the progress of the law suit and was quick to respond when questions came up. I would highly recommend working with Mr. Allaben and his law firm.

Basically was told my case wasn't for their place of business and wasnt refered to someone who possibly could help. Thinking about how I even got refered to them....um yeah should of used my head for more than a hat rack from the start instead of wasting my time.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kisha, we are very sorry we could not assist you after discussing the matter with you. We would urge you to contact other lawyers for further opinions. We do wish you the best.

reviews.text: Mr Bandeen took me on when other law firms said my case was too complicated. Over the course of nearly a year we became a good team. He was bold, but fair, and never said “I can’t’. I can’t recommend his services enough. An all around great guy. Highly recommend.

Randy was very easy to work with. Answered all my questions. I felt at ease knowing he was my legal representation. Very happy with the results... I would highly recommend Randy to Freinds and Family.  Thank you Randy and your freindly staff

Allaben & Bandeen Attorneys is caring and personal with every client, and thorough as well. I would recommend.

He is very easy to talk to and has went above and beyond in my case . He got me a good settlement and I am so grateful . Thank you Bill for everything that you have done.

I like that, because he's going to know what to do better than me 😊 he sends me a message whenever something new is going on with the case and we have a phone call or meeting whenever necessary 😊 the case is moving along quickly so I know he is doing a great job 😊 I was impressed when I went into the building too. It's really nice in there 😊 Joe Gavin is a great lawyer. So if you need one you should go see him 😊
If you ever need a good lawyer who is respectful to you and will dedicate themselves to helping you out this is the place to go to.

The two credit counseling courses were well worth it and I’ll be taking away lessons that will serve me well into the future.  Many thanks :) I feel so very fortunate to have a fresh financial start and will make much better choices going forward

Jason Kara was awesome! He was very patient with me
He helped me in anyway I needed
I would use there services again if need to. If your filing bankruptcy you won't go wrong using this firm.
Jason and Robert were quick and efficient. They spoke to me about everything they were doing. They made the process as easy as possible. I highly recommend Geraci Law
Jason Kara was very knowledgeable, helpful and answered all my questions during the entire process.  I recommend this service to anyone.

reviews.text: Great attorney!

Mr. Grimm took my insurance claim case after another attorney determined it was too far out of the scope of her litigation capabilities.  Never deterred, Steve proceeded through the case with excellent and friendly communication.  Jon Coretti joined him on the prosecution team, and together I was in good hands when opposing the insurance company that was negligent in their handling of my claim.  Thanks to them, the outcome was fair and as expedient as possible.  The best part was the peace of  mind they gave me, along with both of them just being pleasant and, dare I say, fun to be around while in the midst of an extremely stressful and at times desperate situation for me.  They didn't just go through the motions, they displayed genuine investment in the outcome as attorneys who believed they were responsible for righting a wrong, and I am so grateful they took my case.  The rapport they built with me as their client, while being assertive with the opposition as necessary is the best combination anyone can ask for and I can't recommend them enough.

This guy went above and beyond and if you think you have a malpractice with any professional person he will not only be able to tell you if you do or not he will get you top dollar I promise thank you again sir!

Seriously. Thank you man.
And thanks Stephanie!

reviews.text: I was involved in litigation with a very large US based insurance company. A company with a huge legal department. Steve took on the company and I prevailed. Steve took the time to completly understand my position, and then presented my circumstances in a way that they not only withdrew their claim, but did so in less than 3 business days. I have engaged Steve in the past, and certainly would again in the future.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Brad. We certainly slayed Goliath.

reviews.text: I had an absolutely horrible experience with Steve Grimm and his office. I went to him back in May 2016 about a malpractice case and he took it and seemed very willing and anxious to fight for us. There was such a huge lack of communication on his part and I was constantly emailing to ask for updates and no responses. I just received an email today stating that he would not be taking my case...after my attorney had been meeting with him, sending over information, and billing us for the time spent investing into this . I am absolutely horrified with this experience and would discourage ANYONE from working with this law office. Pure disgust!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: You came to me with a legal malpractice claim regarding a very complicated matter that was handled by your previous attorney.  I performed a thorough investigation, and contacted other attorneys, including the one who referred you to me, and was unable to obtain a favorable opinion regarding malpractice. As I mentioned to you several times, a legal malpractice claim must be supported by expert testimony.  After exhausting my investigation, I told you that I could not help you, and advised you to seek the opinion of another attorney immediately.  I told you I would retain your file until I heard from you as to where to send it. I strive to provide clear and concise communication with all of my clients as I review their cases. Looking back through emails and letters with you, this was done in your case. I am truly sorry that you were not happy with the outcome and If you would like to discuss this further please feel free to reach out.

reviews.text: Unfortunately, choosing no stars isn't an option. I reached out to them via their contact form on their website with no response. So, after a few days passed, I called. The person answering the phone said he would look into my website contact as he can't see them. It's been over a week & NO ONE has called me back.
They took good care of me. I would recommend to friends and family.

I had a great experience with the Firm.My attorney was Nick Romer he is very responsive and helpful to make sure everything is done correctly.Helped get my process done quickly. I recommend him if you need a attorney for Disability.

I called because I was having issues with a landlord/tenant. I had called 10+ firms before them and all said they only side with landlords. Then I met Mr. Bradtke! I explained my situation, and was able to berate him with questions. Not only was he super helpful, he took time out of his day to reach out to other firms who have helped people in the same situation as I was going through. He called me back later the same day to tell me what options were. He didn’t even charge me for the help or the time he took out of his day. I HIGHLY recommend using them for any case you may have. Extremely professional, and willing to listen to people. Thank you so much for your help!!
I Kathy Vanderwilp highly recommend this firm. Atty Nicholas Romer was easy to talk to, knowledgeable of the law, and compassionate about sensitive issues. Win win!

I chose this firm because of their great reputation. I was not disappointed.
I cannot say enough good things about this firm. Such great attorneys, and the staff is excellent!

My ex-husband had hired Kari to help him manage years of back taxes. When we divorced, Kari was a strong and encouraging advocate to me, in filing Proof of Innocence paperwork to get me released from my ex-husbands tax debt responsibility. It took lots of expertise, time and perseverance but Kari worked very hard on my behalf until I was finally released of all that debt. She kept me informed every step of the way and I'd highly recommend her.

I can Honestly say that Mr. Murphy is hands down the worst and most incompetent attorney that I have ever come into contact with in my life! He is at best, a glorified email specialist and document preparer. He is weak, soft and slow to act in areas that you need legal representation to be tough harsh and quick to respond! He is  majorly out of touch with modern technology, he is forgetful and sets up meetings to go over things that have been discussed at length multiple times and then sends a bill that is completely outrageous and never gets any results! Please .. Please >> whatever you do, do not hire anyone from this firm. Learn from my mistake, I hired them (Mr. Murphy) to handle my divorce case and after being given terrible council and almost a year of battling and getting absolutely nowhere I had to fire him and hire new council that was able to settle my case within 2 months. If you would like to be billed out over $45,000 in attorney fees to get absolutely nothing accomplished, then Miles Murphy is the guy for the job but if not I'd look elsewhere.

I definitely give 5 stars and highly recommend Reeder Murphy. Their professionalism compassion and understanding when our family dealt with guardianship made the situation easier. Miles and Julie kept us informed every step of the process and were very easy to communicate with. They definitely care about the people the represent.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you immensely for your kind words about Miles and Julie's compassionate support during your guardianship process. We're glad you had a great experience with our firm!

reviews.text: If you want excellent legal help from a personable team, look no further than Reeder Murphy. They specialize in a wide variety of legal matters, and everyone on staff genuinely cares for the clients; so you’ll always be in trustworthy, capable hands. My husband and I wanted to establish an estate plan before welcoming our first baby, and Rick Conklin made the whole process smooth and simple. He helped us feel confident and protected in our medical and financial decisions, and put our minds at ease knowing - no matter what - our son’s needs would always be provided for, and our religious beliefs would be honored, should dire circumstances arise. On another occasion, following an unexpected family emergency, we sought elder care advice; and Jeffrey Black and Rick Conklin promptly and thoroughly discussed our options to protect our relative’s assets. I can vouch for the team’s expert business advice, and I also know they achieve resolutions in probate, family law, and tax matters. I can truly recommend anyone on their team, which I believe says a lot about the integrity of the firm overall. Even if RM doesn’t specialize in your situation, they likely have a referral for you. So, whatever assistance you need, give RM a call; because their clients aren’t just “business” - they are individuals who matter, personally. While no one hopes to need legal counsel, I know I can trust the RM team to be there if (and when) I do, and I can’t thank them enough!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience with Reeder Murphy, P.C. We're glad to hear that our team was able to assist you with your legal needs and that you had a 5.0 star experience with us!

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for recommending Rick Conklin and for trusting Reeder Murphy, P.C. with your legal needs, Holly! We're glad to hear that he was able to assist you and your mother during a difficult time.

He untangled the mess from the other firm and shared a path forward. All of my questions were answered promptly and with legal documentation as a backup. The process became much more clear for me as a trustee to follow.
I remember telling Mr. Black that I would rather pay a higher hourly rate to someone who is efficient, competent, and responsive than pay a lower rate to someone who has none or few of the characteristics listed above.
I would highly, highly recommend this attorney and firm to those who need legal counsel.

reviews.text: I've known attorneys Plachta and Murphy since grade school. I trust both with any legal advice I might need. PMA is a professional and friendly law firm that really does care about its clients' needs. I highly recommend them.

Jeffrey Black  was very knowledgeable and always available.He was very responsive to any questions our family had. He was a class act helping with my grandmothers estate. He made you feel very confident and comfortable in the court room. If your looking for a very professional law firm I would highly recommend them. They are a very good choice. Thanks for everything Jeff.

I have been working with PMA, specifically Kari Thames, for years in regards to my business taxes. She has provided knowledge and expertise with every situation that I have brought to her and has helped me tremendously.

If you're looking for a firm that excels in the services they provide, I would highly recommend Plachta, Murphy & Associates and Kari Thames!
Can not express the appreciation i have for mr bert
Thoroughly impressed by the representation that I received.  Bert and Brian did an excellent job with my business buyout and I would highly recommend them.

Plachta, Murphy and Associates and specifically, Brian Plachta have been assisting me with my legal needs for nearly ten years.  I deeply appreciate the professionalism, accuracy, solid legal advice and the level of service from Mr. Plachta's associates.  I would highly recommend this law firm for anyone seeking legal advice and assistance.
My husband and I had the opportunity to work with Bert Irwin. We found him to be honest, realistic, and personable. His integrity is second-to-none. We highly recommend him without reservation.

We were looking for a local attorney to draw up a land contract and I had posted in the Rockford Services FB page and Bert Irwin reached out to me that he'd be happy to help. I called a total of 5 different attorneys and when I spoke to Bert it was like a breath of fresh air. He was so kind, so professional and really helped me to understand how the land contract process works. I had spoken to an attorney at another firm that was incredibly crass and wouldn't tell me a set fee or number of hours. Bert was straight forward and we were happy to decide on Plachta, Murphey & Associates! That kindness and professionalism carried through our entire land contract and I wouldn't hesitate recommending this firm to everyone I know needing legal services! I can't speak to the other attorneys at the firm, but I have a feeling that they all share similar client values and professionalism! Thank you for a great, pain free legal experience!

We were interested in creating a Living Trust and have known and worked with Brian Plachta over twenty years.  Our meetings and conversations with Brian were helpful and we were given the time we needed to weigh our options.  We never felt rushed, and no question or concern we may have had was ever overlooked.  We were given solid guidance as we moved forward in the process,  Lisa Peterson, legal assistant, also was of great assistance to us.  We highly recommend  this firm for personal and professional excellence in legal matters.

Jeff Black was recommended to us through a friend. We were looking to setup our estate plan. Jeff and his team were great to work with. He made the estate planning process easy to understand and easy to setup.  Everyone at PMA was very friendly and helpful.  I highly recommend them.

We were searching for a law firm to guide us in estate planning. A family member recommended Jeff Black at Plachta, Murphy and Associates, PC and we could not have been more pleased. We liked that it was a smaller firm. The staff was professional and made us feel comfortable and valued. We appreciated Jeff’s ability to listen, offer sound advice and expertise and then create a will and trust to protect our assets. We highly recommend Jeff.

One of the most difficult challenges I've been faced with in many years, was being appointed the personal representative for an estate.
I found this job to an extremely comfortable one, due to the assistance of Bryan Reeder and Jeffrey Black, of the law firm of Plachta, Murphy, and Associates. They professionally and compassionately guided me throughout a very lengthy and complicated estate, lasting over two years. During the entire process, Bryan and Jeffrey were attentive and helpful to all my questions and phone calls, instructing and assisting me step-by-step.
My complete trust and confidence in their law firm gave me a sense of well being during a difficult time. In order for me to complete my responsibilities as a personal representative, this was imperative to me.
I would highly recommend Plachta, Murphy, and Associates for their expert legal services. It was a pleasure working with them.

Cheryl S Gibbs

Although no one relishes the idea of seeking legal advice, life sometimes happens and we do need that counsel. I have needed to seek that counsel twice and both times I chose Platcha, Murphy, & Associates, P.C. Both times this law firm exceeded my expectations. The entire staff was always courteous, professional, friendly, nonjudgemental. The attorneys who represented me (Bryan Reeder the first time and Bert Irwin the second) were outstanding. They listened to me, gave me wise counsel, and represented me and my interests to the highest level of professionalism.

This firm will always be my first choice should I ever require legal counsel in the future. They have a heartfelt, solid 5-star rating from me! I know I can count on them.
Very nice and professional.

Represented me twice ! Both times successfully dismissed. I recommend hiring Denise she’s attentive smart patient and caring. All that in a nutshell she is there to get the results you want and deserve ! Thank you Denise

-Johnny S.
Denise buswa is my go to for anything legal!!!

I used Mr. Berry to defend me when charged with a crime i did not commit.  We paid his deposit.  Very poor communication on his part.   He had very little advice to offer.  He suggested I pled guilty TO A CRIME I DID NOT COMMIT.  The prosecutor withheld evidence I had every right to review.  Mr. Berry did not have enough of a backbone to demand the evidence.  He advised me to accept a plea for something I did not do.  They scheduled another court date.   Mathew Berry DID NOT SHOW UP.   DID NOT ANSWER MY CALLS.  DID NOT ANSWER THE COURTS WHEN THEY CALLED.  I stood in court BY MYSELF as the judge and prosecutor questioned me on WHO Mr. Berry was and WHERE he was.  They posponed as long as they could for me so we could HOPE Mr. Berry would make contact.  He never did.  So we where forced to reschedule.  Three hours later when I was rescheduling he finally responded by TEXT.  His EXCUSE was that he had hurt himself.  His "assistant" was instructed to notify the courts.   Which they did not do.   And that was a LIE cause he dont even have an assistant.  The next court date came and MR. Berry was kind enough to bless us with his presence.   I pled no contest to A CRIME I DID NOT COMMIT.  I was given probation and $750 in fines.
I know 3 others who have used Mathew Berry to represent them in court.  One had a positive outcome.   The other two both said the same thing.   He does not answer or return calls.  He is very quiet and TIMID.  Both stated regret in hiring him as their attorney.  Both had to pay judgements in the end. In my opinion Mr. Berry is good for paperwork only.   He doesn't have the BERRYS to represent ANYONE in court.  At this time I owe Mr. Berry a balance.  I would like HIM to start his NEGOTIATIONS at 66% of what is owed.   Due to the fact he showed up 66% of the time I needed him to.
My advise to Mathew Berry is to aquire the services of a qualified experienced TALENTED lawyer to help him collect his past due accounts.  I am confident I have given him BETTER legal advice than he EVER gave me!

*update*
I left the same review on facebook.   Since then the buswa & berry facebook page has been taken down.

Mr. Berry showed exceptional expertise even with a lot of unknowns during COVID as he helped me through my divorce.  He answered all my questions and was there for me from the beginning.  Mr. Berry offers great service at a very reasonable price.  He truly goes above and beyond for his clients.
Because every time call I get no answer it go's strate to voice mail.

Denise Buswa is a very highly skilled family law attorney. She is also an excellent criminal defense attorney.  I have referred several clients to Denise and I will continue to do so. She is a zealous advocate for her clients and I would highly recommend Denise to anyone seeking counsel.
Matt Berry is a consummate professional. He is knowledgeable and responsive to communication. I would recommend him to anyone for their legal needs.
Matt (Berry) is a great lawyer, extremely professional. I would recommend him to anyone that asked me.

I hired Denise Buswa in a custody case and she did great on getting a lot of what I asked for, it's not perfect but what is right. I don't know how she did it but I'm grateful for the way things turn out. I will be using Denise Buswa again in the future. In a custody case I would recommend her highly.

If you need help with any legal needs this is the place to go DeniseBaswa is an awesome attorney. I've been battling for year's but with the help of Buswa & Berry it is a dream come true. Thank you Buswa & Berry

I rescently hired Denis Buswa as my attorney to help in a texting and driving case. The reason I got an attorney was because I was driving Lyft while between jobs and I couldn't afford to lose my one form of income. The lack of attention to detail was wild. The first time we went into court she didn't even know there was video from the officer. At this time she told me if had to go to trial we would have a good shot at winning because I 100% did not do it. I had records showing I did not text. I had records showing I was not on apps or using data. The day of court at 10:30am I receive a text from Denise asking for my verizon phone bills for the texting and driving case. This was alarming because I had provided all information weeks prior and we had to be in court at 3:30pm. When I got to court I was told by my lawyer that we could not use any of the documents I had provided because she would have had to subpoena verizon and it would have taken a long time (this is what I paid her to do and she showed up with nothing to use).  Furthermore we then discussed a plan of action if we were to go into the trial. She did not use one question that we discussed prior to going into court. She did not have questions prepared, and she was writing them down while we were waiting for trial minutes before we went in. After going through the trial I lost. Denise even said she didnt know how she felt going into count... once again not good to have your lawyer worried about the case minutes before it happens when you provided her with everything to win. Denise also told me I wouldnt have to pay the whole fine if I lost. Once again wrong. If I admitted I was wrong, which I wasn't, and I had the documents to prove it.. I could have walked away with just the fine of 200 dollars. Because I hired Denise and I walked away with a 720 dollar lesson. The sad part was when at the end of it all she asked me if she gave me 100 dollars back would it help since we just lost. I then told her I believe she should at least pay my fine.  She told me she would talk to her partner and I have reached out 4 times now with no responses. She could have won this case. She could have done her job. She is a nice person and this is not personal it's strictly business. If you are looking for a lawyer for anything more serious than a texting and driving case I would truly look somewhere else because this case should have been a layup.

Denise is a highly experienced and compassionate attorney. Her extensive background, particularly in criminal and family law, make her a strong advocate both in the courtroom and in negotiations. She is passionate about the practice of law and about helping her clients achieve the best outcome. I recommend her!

Matt Berry is diligent, hard-working, and has a great ability to make anyone understand the legalities behind any issues. I consulted with him on a business dispute related to non-payment for services, and he was very helpful.

We needed a temporary power of attorney document for my husband to sell some of our property (I had to work on the day of the closing) and Matt Berry went above and beyond to make sure that we had it quickly with all the relevant details. Closing went smoothly and on time.

I waited 7 days for a conflict of interest search to be done by attorney Mckenna Rivers while she held my retainer. I called daily and she never responded to my emails and phone calls then finally received an email stating they could mot represent me with no explanation. This should take a maximum of 24hrs.  Do not bother with this firm.
Thanks for all the help.
Had dealings with two different Attorneys for different reasons & both Gentlemen were extremely helpful!
Will talk about your case to other clients. Very unprofessional and clearly doesn’t believe in client lawyer privacy.
Crooks.  Bad advice, their only directive is billing unneccesary hours.

I recently hired the firm of Foster Swift. I don't think I could have asked for a harder wrking firm. My atty Scott Hogan never seemed to tire w my unending questions. I'd give this 10 stars if I could.  So very glad I chose them. I salute you all

I am extremely disappointed by this firm’s billing practices and would strongly caution others before engaging them.

I was a client of this firm 4–5 years ago for my own estate planning. Recently, I sent a single email inquiry asking about the process and cost for estate planning for my father. That is all. I did not ask to proceed, retain the firm, or authorize any legal work.

Despite this, I was later billed over $300 simply for an attorney to read my email. The firm also generated additional internal work that I never requested, and I was informed I would be charged over $600 in total because of that unsolicited work. I never responded to their follow-up emails and never consented to moving forward.

After I objected, the firm eventually “forgave” the additional charge—but only after I had agreed to pay the $300 fee. That does not make the situation acceptable. Billing hundreds of dollars for reading an inquiry email—especially when no services were authorized—is unreasonable and alarming.

When I was their actual client years ago, there was similar excessive billing practices.

In my experience, this firm operates with extremely aggressive and non-transparent billing practices. Clients should be very cautious, particularly when making what they believe to be a simple informational inquiry. I regret contacting this firm and would not do so again.

David Hill is a top performing Attorney, and we have been thoroughly impressed with his efficiency and processing with our Clients.  He is excellent with his process and knowledge of making a smooth transition.

As a real estate attorney in Grand Rapids, Michigan, David C. Hill at Barnes & Thornburg was a great help in responding to an offer to buy property that my husband and I own in a nearby suburb
He also helped with preparation of a counter offer, and saved us from considerable grief
Moreover, we considered his charges quite reasonable
Belinda Vrielink
Clean and professional building
My mom works in this building

Josh was very Professional excellent to work with. Handled all my calls whenever I needed to speak to him he made sure he found time. Text me back. You don't find lawyers like that Muskegon. Well worth the money! I will use him as much as possible every time thank you Josh.

Absolutely horrible.
Hired them with the agreement to review and ordinance for interpretation and give me options.  Gave a retainer for this of 3K in which we discussed may not even take that amount and he would get back to me after a few days. Was ghosted for weeks after that and when I called the office to get feedback got a rude lecture from the admin about giving me updates. After over a month, FINALLY got a call from Scott and he said he wanted to talk to the township to better understand the issues. Ok. Still didn't get what we agreed upon.  Ghosted again.  No return call before going to court with the township. He actually showed up and told me to just comply. The whole reason I hired him was to tell me my options TO COMPLY.

THEN CHARGED ME 5K for this!
FOR WHAT?

Total rip off and would not suggest. Was 2K over the max agreed upon amount to be ghosted and not get what i hired them for.
Chris is an amazing attorney and I HIGHLY recommend him to anyone! Super caring and responsive. Treats you like family

Filed a PPO on an individual, and they hired Josh as their lawyer. When Josh sent me documentations with their appeal, he gave me the wrong time and day for the appeal hearing. His documentations advised January 7 via Zoom, but the court date was actually mid December in person. I was not notified properly of the hearing and it was too late to appeal the decision since it was more than 2 weeks when I realized that he gave me the wrong court date. Seems he’s a little shady when it comes to these things to assure he ‘wins’ his cases. 🤦🏽‍♀️

He explained the risks and rewards of my various options and informed me of the costs upfront. It was like we were sitting on the same side of the table trying to find the best solution to the issue at hand. He was very responsive, even replying on weekends.

Used Jason in a construction case and would highly recommend him to anyone. He was easy to work with and the other guy was a psycho. Jason won us the case and really knows his stuff. Would highly recommend him to anyone.

I was referred to Thomas Kuiper by a friend and reached out to him with a few questions about a family trust. reviews.text:
No conscience in this firm.  Christian simply abandoned me because he didnt like my questions.  Had to retain a new lawyer weeks before the divorce was to be finalized costing me an additional $45000 to retrace and proceed.

Please research all reviews, you will see my complaints are not an anomaly.  There are more one star reviews than they can justify.

Got my day in small claims.  Dont let them bully you. Furthermore they have recently threatened me with legal action for my review.  As you continue to read their reviews, you will see I was not the only one threatened.

In fact you will see that more people have responded positively to the 1 star reviews than the endless 4 star reviews that seem to clutter out a disturbing profile.  Thus i assume there may be other clients who cannot post dissatisfied reviews for fear of "discovery" or financial terrorism.

Honestly, they didnt even try their case against me in court. They capitulated.  Completely.  They had no work products to justify the charges.

Most pathetically, George, the father, represented Christian's allegations in a small claims courtroom, a space in which citizens are not allowed legal representation.

Sad but true. I was facing two attorneys with no one to back up little brown me.  Ha!

Paid what I had originally proposed to them - in full which was  $1200, including costs $1355.   They sued me for over $5000.  Check their numbers and then check mine.  Dont owe them a penny.  Still to receive a notice of satisfaction of judgement.

They actually owe me $100.00.  Have the threats and receipts documented if you are a curious citizen.

This is Public Record: Krupp vs Samaddar.  Dont trust these reviews.

Do u really want to hire a father and son team you can beat yourself in court by googling relevant law?

No shame in their game.

reviews.text: If given the opportunity to choose an attorney again I would strongly suggest a different law firm representing me. Nothing went right. For months I would receive no calls letting me know the status of my case, and when I would call Van Den Huevel Law, they would charge me fees to tell me that they haven't heard anything from the court. Finally, after 19 months, and five months before my previous call to them, I called again. When I called it seemed as though they were surprised to hear from me as they had forgotten my case. The next day they called me. This is a first! They called me. They told me my case finally has a date set for a hearing in just a couple weeks. Great! I finally have my day. However, on that day before the court in a video hearing the judge asked Howard why it is that his office had not contacted the court about this case. Howard in his defense, not my defense said that he had been waiting for the courts to contact him. And this continued on for several minutes before returning to focus on the purpose of that days hearing in which the judge confirmed it had been too long a period and that his case with the court is dismissed without prejudice. I was very appalled with this revelation. After the hearing I was asked by Howard if I would like to appeal the courts decision and bring my case to the appeals court of Michigan. Wow! I couldn't believe that this man who just failed me bold fased in front of a judge would ask for me to let him represent me further. In appeals court. I told him NO, and I will talk to him further after at a later time. One week later I received a letter from the court letting me know my date for a hearing in appeals court. ONE WEEK LATER I RECEIVED A LETTER FROM THE COURT LETTING ME KNOW MY DATE FOR AN APPEALS HEARING. WHAT!?! I immediately contacted his office telling them to stop with the appeal, in which they advised me that I needed to come to their office and sign a paper that relieves them of any further duties. All of which they continued to charge me fees for, INCLUDING THE APPEAL THEY FILED WITH THE COURTWHICH I NEVER WANTED AFTER HEARING AND SEEING THE JUDGE REPREMAND HOWARD FOR INCOMPETANCE. Why would I want an attorney who failed me continue to represent me? There is no chance in hell I would again get treated the way I did by this firm. I continued to fight against the additional charges/fees applied to me for them filing an appeal. Five years later. I don't understand such a breakdown in communication.

Had a phone consultation with Howard. He gave me the run down on how his office works and an introduction to his team. Answered my questions, assuring me about my case. Very polite and respectful. Excellent legal advice.

Howard was so kind and genuinely passionate about helping me. He took the time to share a wealth of valuable information and made me feel completely supported and informed. I would absolutely recommend Howard and his wonderful team.

Several months ago I had a consultation with Howard Van Den Heuvel when seeking an attorney. Unfortunately, he was not able to represent my case since the matter required I hire an attorney in Virginia, due to the very state and regionally specific laws relevant to my issue. Despite that, Howard was very generous and kind in offering general advice and encouragement that helped me proceed, and resources for seeking out an attorney in the appropriate region. If a legal need ever arises closer to home, he will be the first person I call. He earned my trust and confidence quickly, and is clearly a kind man of high knowledge, moral standards, and integrity. I recently recommended him to a friend, and would recommend him to anyone who needs legal counsel, without hesitation.

The Van Den Heuvel team has taken care of me for almost 2 years now, and got me through the family court system with very satisfactory results.

They did an amazing job preparing me every step of the way, keeping me level headed, keeping expectations realistic, coached me on the things I needed, were extremely organized, and charismatic. It is very clear they are in the know, are respected in their profession and other professionals always seemed to enjoy interacting with them.

This is a business, they are really fair but precise. They are very clear and transparent with their billing, it's well documented, and I always have access to this information. I am not wealthy, these expenses were hard on me, but where they have taken me and the results I recieved I feel very good about it. Money WELL spent.

Key notes:
Both Howard and Justin handled my case and appearances. They are very similar but each has their own distinct methods, both are very effective.
Most importantly, with the advise I was given, I was able to load them up with very solid, large amounts of documentation and had good communication. They took this ammo, it was well filtered, well organized, pin pointed and highlighted. They showed up with suchan overwhelming amount of information, the other side got tired of responding. Once she was taken care of, the logistics of managing insurance, the bills, and process was overwhelming. We were being bullied by multiple institutions. The Humane Society pointed us to Melissa at Gruel Mills, and that was the best referral we could have gotten.

From the start, Melissa outlined the path forward and the steps...exactly what needed to happen with bills, insurance, therapy, treatment, negotiations process and what we could expect. After we settled the case, what stood out the most was how much Melissa fought for us, maintained consistent communication even during massive insurance pushback, and balanced the genuine care for our daughter. The check-in's, updates, never left us wondering what was going on.

If you want someone who is both sharp and compassionate, someone you can actually trust to get the job done, I recommend Melissa without hesitation.

reviews.text: Over 3-1/2 years ago, my adult son and family were in a catastrophic auto accident, as a result of a drunk driver. My son, the sole survivor, and the owner of a business was severely injured and in a coma. Our world as we knew it changed in a moment. I was bombarded with requests for interviews from the media, attorneys wanting to represent this case, dealing with the insurance company, medical decisions for my son as well as end of life arrangements for his family. On the strong recommendation of my son’s business attorney, I contacted Paul Janes. He met with me immediately. The very first step that I needed to take was to become my son’s guardian and conservator, so that I could legally have access to “his life”, make decisions for him, his family and business. Paul’s expertise is unmatched in his profession! I found Paul to be very professional, respectful and caring about his clients. We have learned that as you start to physically recover from an auto accident tragedy, it is just the beginning of the fight for your life! Paul has demonstrated that he has expert knowledge of Michigan No-fault Law, fierce tenacity and toughness needed to deal with complex insurance challenges. Communication, one of Paul’s many strengths has been invaluable to me. I was able to count on receiving very timely responses from him, which was crucial when being faced with so many decisions. I would highly recommend Paul to anyone that is looking for a stellar attorney of integrity that you can trust to guide you through the tumultuous waters of a tragic motor vehicle crash.  Paul, thank you! I am very grateful for all of your care and support. Also, a huge thank you to Paul’s team – Carol, Mindy and Amanda – you are all priceless! Betsy Ormsbee

I would Highly Recommend GMNP!  Attorney J. Paul Janes has been my attorney for 3 years, representing me in my tragic automobile accident.  Paul practices a special brand of council, I heard early on "that's Paul and only he can do that in his Paul Janes way".  I quickly learned what that meant: 5 Star communication, being there every time myself or my family needed him.  I'm blessed to having got to know him and even more blessed to call him friend.  Last but certainly not least, Pauls support staff is also Rock Star!  Thank you Mindy, Amanda and Carol for all you professionalism and hours on my behalf!

reviews.ownerResponse: Due to confidentiality, we are not at liberty to disclose any details related to a conversation with a potential client and/or his or her medical care.  However, we treat every call seriously and attempt to answer any questions and provide explanation for any decision when we are unable to pursue a legal action on an individual’s behalf.  If Mr. Adegboye or any individual has questions related to his or her potential case, we hope they would be willing to call or set up a meeting to discuss the matter further.  As Mr. Adegboye’s friends and the referring attorney have recognized, we take our clients’ or potential clients’ cases very seriously.  The quality of our clients’ representation and our reputation are extremely important to us.  We will not, however, knowing put any individual through the trauma and expense of a lengthy legal action, if we are not confident that there is a meritorious claim and that the individual will be substantially better off at the end of the process than he/she was before the process began.

Unfortunately, we were placed with a lawyer from the opposite end of the spectrum when our insurance company hired James Crocker from this company to drill us to make sure we did not set our own home on fire while we were 7 months pregnant and after the police already had cleared us. reviews.text:
I had the privilege of working with Grand Rapids Criminal Defense Lawyer Shawn Haff during a very stressful and uncertain time, and I can confidently say he is among the best criminal defense attorneys you could hope for in West Michigan. For anyone in or around Grand Rapids facing criminal charges—whether DUI, domestic violence, white-collar crime, Retail Fraud, drug charges or something more complex—Mr. Haff offers a combination of skill, compassion, and aggressive advocacy that truly sets him apart.

From our first meeting, Shawn made it clear that I wasn’t “just another case.” He listened patiently, answered every question in plain language, and never made me feel judged for being in a tough spot. His website says it plainly: “I understand that hard working and good people may have made a bad choice. I won’t judge you. I am here to help you!”

That personal touch matters—especially when you’re navigating a system that can feel overwhelming and intimidating.

It became clear to me early on that Shawn doesn’t just “handle” cases—he fights them. He thoroughly investigated the facts of my situation, explained the strengths and weaknesses of the prosecution’s position, and built a clear strategy. In my case resolution, I received far better terms than I initially expected—and I credit that entirely to his tenacity.

One of the most refreshing aspects of working with Shawn: from the beginning he laid out fees, possible outcomes, and what to expect—not what might be wishful thinking. I never felt blindsided with hidden charges or unrealistic promises. Many reviews mention the same—he “helped me along the way” and “made me feel at ease knowing how worried I was.”

Anyone in Kent, Ottawa, Allegan, Barry, Van Buren, Newaygo, or other surrounding West Michigan counties facing criminal charges—from misdemeanors to high-stakes felonies can be helped by Shawn's expertise in criminal defense.

Shawn is an advocate, not just a presence in court. Someone who treats the case like it’s personal and dives into the details.

Shawn was always transparent and given me direct guidance, which was important to me, since I was not familiar with how criminal proceedings work, since this was my first offense.

If you’re reading this because you’ve been charged with something—and maybe you’re feeling anxious, ashamed, or unsure of what to do—I can’t recommend Shawn James Haff enough. His blend of legal skill, integrity, and genuine care puts him at the top of the field in Grand Rapids.

Hiring him changed the outcome of my case in a way I wouldn’t have believed possible at the start. If you choose to work with him, you’ll have someone in your corner who will fight hard, explain things clearly, and treat you with respect every step of the way.

Whether you’re only beginning to explore your options or ready to hire an attorney immediately—call Shawn. You’ll be glad you did.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Adam came to me seeking help because he was facing prison time after picking up two felony charges. Thanks to my work, I was able to get Adam an amazing deal that kept him from going to prison and dropped one of the serious felony charges Adam was facing. Now, nearly two years after my work with Adam is done, he proceeds to defame me with obvious false information. A reasonable client would be thanking me for keeping him out of prison, getting him a reduction in charges and leave me a five-star review.

Adam was offered 7411 if he pled guilty to ALL the serious felony charges he was facing. Adam rejected that offer so he was not sentenced under 7411.

After Adam gave his approval to the plea deal I negotiated, we went into court in front of Judge Miedema and Adam pled guilty to only one felony charge instead of the multiple charges he was facing. I was right next to Adam, in front of the judge, when she went over what the plea offer was and Adam said he understood what he was pleading guilty to. The Judge was slow and methodical in taking the plea, so there is simply no way Adam wasn't aware of what he pled guilty to. 7411 was not part of the plea bargain. Everything that happened at court that day is public record. The public transcript clearly shows I am telling the truth and Adam's claims are not true. Finally, the plea agreement with Adam's signature and my signature on it clearly states he will only get 7411 if he entered a guilty plea to ALL the charges.

Adam has never contacted me after his sentencing. Discovery was available anytime he wanted it. After looking over the retainer, Adam even gets the retainer fee incorrect. The retainer was signed in 2023. I never told Adam he could get his record cleaned up immediately after he completed probation. He should be able to get his conviction expunged down the line. I called the Grand Haven Courthouse numerous times checking for any possible warrants against Adam. If I was aware of a warrant out for his arrest, I would have let him know asap. If Adam had taken the plea deal under 7411, he would be done with his probation and not have a record at the moment. Be salty at yourself Adam, not me. I helped give you a chance to keep your record clean, but you refused the offer. I wish you well and hope you make better choices in the future. Shawn

reviews.text:
This was a complete waste of time. I showed up fully prepared with a major, high-value case involving over $900 million in cryptocurrency fraud. I had already done all the legwork myself—completed the investigation, organized the evidence, drafted the exhibits, and had everything ready for submission to the Eastern District of Michigan. She wouldn’t have had to do any heavy lifting—just represent the case.

Despite that, she was over 30 minutes late to our scheduled Zoom consultation and never even showed up to the platform. I sat there waiting while she casually called me by phone instead, acting like blowing off the Zoom meeting was no big deal.

When we finally talked, she didn’t ask serious questions about the case. Instead, she veered off into irrelevant tangents and acted like I didn’t know what I was talking about—even trying to explain crypto basics I clearly understood better than she did. Her knowledge of cryptocurrency law is shallow, and it showed.

Worse, all she seemed to care about was getting paid. I had made it clear ahead of time that I was requesting a contingency arrangement due to the nature of the stolen assets. She kept repeating that she doesn’t take contingency cases, but if that’s true—why take the meeting at all? She clearly didn’t care about wasting my time.

Her tone was condescending, and I left feeling like she thought she was above me just because she’s a lawyer. There was no interest, no respect, and no sense of partnership. Just ego, dismissal, and dollar signs.

If you’re dealing with a serious crypto case and you’ve already done the hard work—look elsewhere. This isn’t someone who values your time, effort, or experience.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Wade, I am sorry that you feel this way. I provided you with a free, 30-minute consultation. I am more than qualified with my experience and an LLM in Homeland and National Security Law to handle your case. Attorneys are required to verify information and investigate allegations before filing a case. However, unfortunately, you did not provide me with any information to review. You requested full contingency payment terms, and that is not something that my firm offers at this time. My assistant informed you of this before our consultation. Before I even quoted you a cost for your case, you decided that you did not want to move forward. At the end of the day, I understand that you are having a difficult time. I wish you well and hope that you find an attorney that is a better fit for you.

Kind Regards,

reviews.text: Yeah. The fact that I’m writing this review shows I have made some mistakes in my life that I am not proud of. That being said, Martin is a seriously compassionate, understanding guy. He responded to my initial email on the Friday before Memorial Day and got to looking into my case of getting an interlock removed. So yes, very responsive. I had the interlock for 3-4 years per the courts and for my personal accountability and strengthening of my sobriety. So, the case got started around Memorial Day. Fast forward 2 weeks, I got a violation stating that your car repair caused your interlock to lose power and some tests were missed. I couldn’t drive for 2 months (after just spending thousands to fix the car I’m required to own to keep restricted privileges). I call his office around midnight leaving a panicked voicemail. 8:30 am he texts and says send me the pages I’ll get started on this right away. Interlock violation cases there are several law firms that won’t even do them because they are harder to win. Martin got me out of the violation hearing in 5 minutes and I was back to restricted. I was very impressed considering he also charges half what the guys with nice websites do. End of July that case was done and August through November I got my stuff in order to fully reinstate my license. I was successful at a hearing in December and on the spot within 15 minutes the hearing officer stated “I earned it.” Martin did the hearing on a day after he had some health concerns. That is purely selfless. He wants to help people and doesn’t solely care about the money. He’s also very entertaining and had me laughing quite a bit, even throughout the rigorous process. Will be referring him or those in his network for any friends with any driving related issues. He knows any possible issue that can happen in a Michigan Driver’s license.

So because of him I now lost my license until 2021...

The judge after the fact even said to me “why did you plead guilty?” Like it was a stupid thing to do..  so if you’re looking for license restoration attorneys or an attorney in general... I’d look for someone with more experience.    As nice of a man as he is, I’m having to suffer five years... even after giving the state of Michigan 12.5 thousand dollars in fees...

reviews.text: DO NOT GO HERE!!!! After 2 years and thousands and thousands of dollars, my dad was told the lawyer wasn’t even going to go to Detroit with him for his meeting with immigration. After getting a new lawyer, she was able to secure his green card in JUST 3 months. The difference between lawyers was night and day. Richard was hard to get ahold of, the staff was be rude, and he refused to show up to crucial meetings with immigration. Richard kept making my dad believe he would not get approved and made him cancel his appointments finally telling him to “marry a citizen”. His new lawyer was sweet, kind, professional, and personable. You could feel her passion and it showed in how quick she gave results.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Yvette and Stephanie,

I want to address your concerns, and will try to do so while respecting your father’s privacy.

Since we are talking about your fathers case, I don't know how much of the specifics you know. As we worked on your father’s case, something in his immigration past came up that I believe would most likely require a special waiver. He was made aware of that . Unfortunately that waiver is only available through marriage to a US citizen and furthermore you need to show hardship to the U.S. citizen spouse. At that time, your father told me he had a serious long-term relationship with a US citizen. I took time to explain the situation thoroughly and inquire with your father and that is why I explained marriage to a US citizen as a possible option to obtain a waiver and advised postponing all upcoming appointments he had with Immigration until he could apply for the waiver. I offered to help him complete the waiver, but since it was an additional procedure to what we had agreed to before, I told him I would have to charge additional fees. We never charged him as much as you said and we put in sustainable work on his case including submitting the first round of documents. Furthermore, as far as the appointment, the issue was that your father wanted to go ahead with an appointment and say certain things that I was not on board with. Therefore, I did not feel comfortable participating in the immigration interview as I often do with clients.

I was also surprised to hear that he felt I was impolite as I make it a point to be polite and friendly with my clients. Your father never complained about my treatment of him.

If another lawyer was really able to get your father his green card so quickly, I am glad, but you should understand that was based on all the filings we made in his case. Although I am concerned that if they did not file a waiver, that he could have problems in the future. If you, with your father’s consent,or your father would like to speak privately with me, I would be happy to explain my concerns again at no charge to you.

reviews.text: Do not let this man scam you. Will not help you. Lack of communication. Very non personable and rude. Took my fathers new lawyer 3 months to do what he did in 2 years. After robbing us of $12,000 he told my father that he could no longer help his case and that he should just marry a citizen. Disgusting!!!

reviews.text:
DO NOT USE THEM FOR MEDICAID APPLICATIONS
Very poor experience, no follow through and insanely overpriced!

Be aware your initial meeting is not with the attorney that will be assigned to you, it is designed to sell you on the fees that appear to be based on the up front financial information you provided them.  The initial meeting has a timeshare pitch feel to it,  "you can't afford not to hire us" tone.
Carrier Law promised my father he would get more than the $31,000 they were charging.  THIRTY ONE THOUSAND to review a trust, wills, do two property transfers and apply for Medicaid!!!
I suspect the fees are based on how much they think you can afford to pay.

From the time they took the $31,000 it took 7 months to apply for Medicaid due to their own incompetence:
1.  They never started on the project for over 6 weeks after they were hired.  Medicaid applications have an urgency,  clearly Carrier Law isn't equipped for urgent Medicaid applications.
2.  They struggled for 5 months to do a simple instate property transfer.
3.  They could not find a Florida attorney to work with them on a property transfer which I find odd since many Michigan residents have property in Florida.  My corporate law firm has never had a problem finding out of state attorney's to work with.  I'm sure there is something they weren't being honest about.
4.  There was a name issue with the property deed and the name of the trust.  My 86 year old father came up with a solution and hired another attorney to fix that since Carrier law couldn't figure out how to remedy the problem.
5.  I highly doubt they would have been able to complete this in 7 months if we wouldn't have nagged them (lots of unreturned phone calls), demanded in person meetings to report on their progress and got belligerent (demanded we talk directly with David Carrier).

7 months into the whole mess they finally applied for Medicaid but my mother had passed by that time so it was a total waste of time.  Carrier Law lead my father to believe that there was no urgency to the Medicaid application and told him to reduce the amount paid to the nursing home as if he was going to receive full benefits.  He did this for the 7 months Carrier Law dinked around with his case.  7 months equaled a $60,000 past due invoice from the nursing home.
In the end they claimed that they never told us Medicaid benefits were retroactive and there is only a small window for retroactive benefits.  Well if that was the case I reiterate the need for them to act urgently.  In other words, they knew if they would have started on the project in a timely manner they may have been able to deliver on their promises.

If they would have done the work they were hired to do my mother would have received $60,000 in Medicaid benefits for the time she was in the nursing home, she received ZERO.  NINETY ONE THOUSAND DOLLAR MISTAKE.   Obviously Medicaid applications and property transfers aren't their area of expertise.

The workshops have been very helpful and informative.  I am glad to have this portion of the estate planning in place.  It is very much a relief. The staff at Carrier Law has been very helpful and friendly.

reviews.text:
When my accident happened, everything changed in an instant. The physical pain was one thing, but the fear and uncertainty were something else entirely. I was trying to stay strong for my son while quietly wondering how I was going to navigate everything ahead of us. It felt overwhelming in ways I can’t fully describe.

Finding Michigan Auto Law gave me hope during one of the darkest and most uncertain times in my life.

From the beginning, Jeremy R. Tiedt showed not only professionalism but genuine compassion. He listened when I needed to talk. He explained things when I felt confused. He reassured me when anxiety started to creep in. I never felt rushed or like “just another case.” I felt respected.

Tom Baker worked alongside him with the same level of care and dedication, making sure every detail was handled thoroughly. And Lori Baird and Tonya Harris were incredible behind the scenes coordinating, communicating, and making sure nothing slipped through the cracks. Their kindness and patience meant more than they probably realize.

This has been a really rough accident, and I will always carry scars and difficult memories. But your kindness and simply being there for me meant the world. During one of the hardest seasons of our lives, you helped give me strength and stability when I needed it most.

As a mother, there is nothing more important to me than protecting my child. Because of your support, I was able to focus on healing and being there for my son instead of carrying the full weight of everything alone. For that, I will always be grateful.

You didn’t just represent my case you helped steady my life when it felt like it was falling apart. Thank you for everything.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much! Your kind words about Michigan Auto Law’s Grand Rapids car accident lawyers Jeremy Tiedt and Tom Baker and legal professionals Lori Baird and Tonya Harris mean the world to us. Nothing makes us more proud than knowing that Jeremy, Tom, Lori and Tonya were able to give you the hope, strength and stability you needed to carry both yourself and your son through this very challenging time. Thank you for putting your trust in Michigan Auto Law. We wish you and your son the absolute very best.

As both a clinician and case manager with over 14 years in the auto no-fault and brain injury space, I have worked alongside many attorneys and firms. This team is, without question and without comparison, is the best auto no-fault attorneys I have ever worked with.

They consistently go above and beyond, not only for me, but for my clients. What truly sets them apart is the mutual respect and trust they extend to the clinical and care-management side of cases. They value my professional judgment as a clinician, brain injury specialist, and ethical provider just as much as I rely on their deep expertise in Michigan auto no-fault law and client rights. That level of collaboration is rare.

They treat my entire team with the same respect and dignity they show me, communicate clearly and promptly, and are always willing to stand firm, even against the most aggressive opposing attorneys, to secure the care, restitution, and representation our clients deserve.

Above all, they are compassionate, ethical, responsive, and deeply committed to the people they serve. Their professionalism is matched by a bedside manner I have never seen in any other law firm.

If you are looking for an auto no-fault attorney who truly understands both the legal and human sides of catastrophic injury cases, this is the team you want.

With true admiration and respect,

We wish we could help everyone who contacts us, and we understand that people can be disappointed when we cannot take their case, but sometimes this means advising someone when pursuing a case is not in their best interests. Our attorneys devote substantial time free of charge to every person who calls us to determine whether a legally viable claim exists. When the facts do not support a case that we can responsibly pursue, it would be unfair both to you and to our current clients to promise otherwise.  We also can see that you are giving both our law firm and all the other personal injury law firms that you have called a 1‑star rating simply because they also are declining to take your case.  This misrepresents the quality of our work and our dedication to extraordinary client care. Our commitment is to honesty and transparency in every interaction, even when sometimes that means saying “no.” If you would like help locating another attorney or free community resources, please call us back and we will gladly point you in the right direction.
